title: Ruth E. Carter Makes Oscar History with Fifth Nomination
text: Ruth E. Carter became the most-nominated Black woman across any Oscar category in the Academy's 96-year history when she secured her fifth nomination for Best Costume Design for Sinners on January 22, 2026. Carter expressed surprise at the milestone, revealing that director Ava DuVernay informed her of the historic achievement. I read it, and I was like, Damn, that's historic, she said, emphasizing that while telling historic stories professionally, she was simultaneously creating her own groundbreaking narrative. The 65-year-old costume designer brought extensive knowledge to the vampire period piece, drawing inspiration from historical photographers and her deep understanding of African American cultural history. I plunged headfirst into James Van Der Zee and the photographers of the times, looking at people and what they wore and how they layered, she explained. Carter previously won Oscars for Black Panther in 2019 and Black Panther: Wakanda Forever in 2023, both directed by Ryan Coogler. Reuniting with Coogler for Sinners, despite its half the budget of the Marvel films, felt meaningful to her. The film itself made history as the most-nominated movie with 16 total nominations, surpassing the previous record of 14 held by All About Eve, Titanic, and La La Land.
